rulings.Case Title: COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC et al (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Commonwealth Land Title Insurance Company'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 1 ( Part 2 of 2). Plaintiff Commonwealth Land Title Insurance Company'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. No judgment may be entered on a part of a cause of action on a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Notice of Stay of Proceedings re: Defendant Emerich on file. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LO et al VS. DANIEL S LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 2. DEFENDANT DEBBIE YOU DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. (1) Defendant demurs to the 9-11th causes of action on several grounds and the Court rules as follows: (a) Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend at to causes of action 9-11th for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that the sum of $90,300 was applied to the 8th Avenue property. If Plaintiff cannot so allege in good faith Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend per Kim v. Regents of Univ. of California, (2000) 80 Cal. App. 4th 160 as stated below. (b) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 9-11th causes of action. These causes of action are barred by the Statute of Limitations and are not subject to relation back doctrine per Kim v. Regents of Univ. of California, (2000) 80 Cal. App. 4th 160. There was nothing in the first two iterations of the complaint concerning Leavenworth and Divisadero properties and there is no indication in the operative complaint or the briefing that transfers involve the same accident and the same instrumentality as the 8th Avenue transfer. Additionally, Plaintiff claims that the "last overt act" of the purported conspiracy is March 21, 2009, the date of Divisadero property sale, i.e. Leavenworth and Divisadero transactions were separate and distinct from 8th Avenue transfer - had the transfers of Leavenworth, Divisadero and 8th Avenue been a part of one accident/instrumentality/conspiracy, the last overt act would have been the transfer of the 8th Avenue property alleged to have occurred on or after the divorce of Lee and You on November 22, 2010. (Complaint, paragraphs 25 and 30). (2) Demurrer is overruled as to the 12th cause of action for accounting. It is sufficiently alleged. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT EICHHORN VS. GRAND PACIFIC HOTEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 3. DEFENDANT GRAND PACIFIC HOTEL, MR. WONG Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support is GRANTED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly label each cause of action asserted; indicate which Defendant a particular cause of action is alleged against; and to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action, including damages. Plaintiff should also indicate the relief sought as to each cause of action. Plaintiff's allegations shall be made in clear and concise language. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FULRUSO LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. BETSY BOGUE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 4. DEFENDANT BETSY BOGUE, CHRIS PERRY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges all causes of action. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: QUINTEN FRY VS. JUSTIN D'ORIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp Section 438)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 5. DEFENDANT JUSTIN D'ORIO Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp Section 438) is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) verify his complaint for quiet title; and (2) allege facts, not conclusions re: discovery of the facts constituting the mistake as in Arthur v. Davis (1981) 126 Cal.app.3d 684, including but not limited to what Fry thought he was signing; why he thought so; how the Grant Deed got recorded and by whom if Fry did not intend to deliver it; and why he did not discover the nature of the document he has signed earlier. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA JOHANNA SAINEZ VS. RENEE A. OLIVAS-CARRASCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Continuance Of Trial And Settlement Conference
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 07, 2017, Line 6. DEFENDANT RENEE OLIVAS-CARRASCO Notice Of Motion And Motion For Continuance Of Trial And Settlement Conference is GRANTED per stipulation of the parties. Trial is continued to June 26, 2017. Settlement Conference to take place on the same day. =(501/REQ)
